threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the tube' instead of 'the Overground' when you're heading to Dalston. It’s a dead giveaway.
Caught the N35 last night; a bloke was blaring Chopin from his phone, completely zoned out while the rest of us squeezed past his bag. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Watching a group of kids turn a vacant lot into a makeshift soccer field. Just pure joy and creativity.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find an open-air cinema screening retro films in an abandoned tram depot. Only in Berlin, right?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who's local by how they can dodge a Muni bus while juggling a burrito and still manage to spit out the latest food truck gossip.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 14 leaving the club at 2 AM. He was explaining Muni routes to a tourist like it was his second job. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all bohemian cafés, but many locals head to the back streets for family-run bodegas with the best tapas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they wander into the 'Carniceria' looking for beef instead of the jamón. A telltale sign for sure.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Overground' and not just 'the orange line.' Also, your flat's still too quiet for a Hackney street.
Last week on the N35, a woman serenaded the driver with a full Whitney Houston ballad. Even the usual drunks sat in awe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw an old man in a bathrobe chasing a runaway dog past a kebab shop, I knew I was home in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find spontaneous art installations popping up by the Spree, like the one at Wiesenweg, with no permits. Just pure Berlin creativity.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name the taqueria on 24th that took over the old laundromat, but tourists still think it's all about the burritos on Mission.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy on the 14 Mission swing his backpack off like it was a dance move. Perfectly timed with the bus lurching to a stop. Classic Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think Gràcia is just about Gaudí and fancy cafés, but the real charm is in the tiny art studios tucked behind those bustling plazas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones at the local café asking for 'un café con leche' in nearly perfect Spanish but still squinting at the menu like it's a puzzle.
